prompt: A comparison graphic on a black background with a faint gray square grid, designed like a social media test result poster. At the top are 2 large side-by-side titles in bold sans serif text: “GPT image 2” above the left image and “Nano Banana” above the right image. Below them are 2 vertically oriented illustrated posters placed next to each other with a small gap. Both posters depict the same concept: a hand-drawn foodie walking map of Guangzhou in Chinese, but rendered in 2 different visual styles. The left poster is more detailed, textured, dense, and richly illustrated, while the right poster is cleaner, flatter, and simpler. The left poster has a warm cream paper background and an intricate vintage hand-painted travel-map style with fine linework, watercolor texture, decorative flowers and lychee fruits, miniature buildings, roads, river areas, and many food illustrations. The large Chinese title at the top of the left poster reads “广州 吃货暴走地图”. The map includes roughly 12 featured food spots distributed across the city, each shown as a small food illustration with Chinese labels and short handwritten-style annotations. Visible foods include skewers, roast poultry, noodle dishes, clay pot dishes, rice rolls, dumplings, soups, and dessert bowls, with landmarks such as the Canton Tower and riverfront scenery integrated into the map. The right poster also has the Chinese title “广州 吃货暴走地图” and shows the same general idea but in a cleaner infographic-like map style with simplified roads, icons, floral and lychee border decorations, food callouts, a river, the Canton Tower, and approximately 12 labeled food illustrations around the map. At the bottom of the overall comparison graphic is 1 rounded black text panel with a white outline. Attached to its upper left edge is 1 bright yellow rounded tab label reading “提示词” in bold black Chinese characters. Inside the black panel is a block of white Chinese text describing the image-generation prompt; it appears as a multi-line paragraph. The composition is centered, high contrast, and presented as a polished AI image comparison post for Chinese social media, with crisp typography, poster-like symmetry, and a clean test-benchmark aesthetic.
